messages.text: This is it. We move for the summit tonight.
Every step counts from here. Follow my pace.
Watch each other. Altitude affects judgment before it affects the body.
The whole team back home is with you today.

system_card.heading: ZONE · DEATH ZONE
system_card.body: The team enters the altitude zone above 8,000 m where oxygen is critically low.

messages.text: A teammate is showing early signs of altitude sickness. What do we do?

options.label: Push on toward the summit — we're close.

reaction.text: That's exactly how emergencies turn fatal up here. We turn back for them, now.
reaction.low: No. That call would kill someone. We turn back for them, now.
reaction.high: I know how close we are — I really do — but that's how emergencies turn fatal. Please, turn back for them.

options.label: Turn the entire team back immediately.

reaction.text: Caution is right, but let's assess before we give up the summit for everyone.
reaction.low: Cautious. Let's actually assess before we throw away the summit for everyone.
reaction.high: I respect the instinct — let's still assess before we give up the summit for everyone.

options.label: Have Maya assess immediately and decide based on her read.

reaction.text: Thank you for trusting the medical call. That's exactly how this should work.
reaction.low: Sensible. Let me actually do my job now.
reaction.high: Thank you for trusting me on the medical call. Honestly, that's the leader I hoped you'd become.

messages.text: This is the window we waited for. Stay close and steady.

system_card.heading: FEED · SUMMIT WINDOW OPEN
system_card.body: Two new items are visible in the Weather & Route tab.
metrics: WX ALERT · Winds calm, clear visibility · LOW
SUMMIT BULLETIN · Cleared to proceed · HIGH

messages.text: We're within reach, but the window is narrow. How do we make this final push?

options.label: Move as fast as possible to beat the window.

reaction.text: Rushing here risks everything we've built. Slow down.
reaction.low: No. Rushing here throws away everything we've built. Slow down.
reaction.high: I know — the window is there — but rushing risks everything we've built. Slow down.

options.label: Hold position and reassess conditions again.

reaction.text: We've reassessed enough. This is the moment to commit.
reaction.high: I hear the caution — I respect it — but we've reassessed enough. Commit with me.

options.label: Advance together at Tenzin's steady, disciplined pace.

reaction.text: That's how we reach the top. Together, and in control.
reaction.low: Acceptable. Let's see if the team actually holds the pace.
reaction.high: That's how we reach the top — together, in control. I'm proud to rope with you.

messages.text: We made it. Take a moment — you've earned it.
Everyone is stable. That matters more than the summit itself.
This is why we prepare the way we do.
The whole team will want to hear this story.
The summit is only half the mission. How do we approach the descent?

options.label: Descend quickly — the hard part is over.

reaction.text: Most accidents happen on descent. The mission isn't over yet.
reaction.low: No. Most accidents happen on descent. The mission is not over.
reaction.high: I know it feels done — but most accidents happen on descent. Please, hold the pace.

reaction.text: Rest matters, but our supply window is also closing.
reaction.low: Rest is fine. Our supply window isn't. I'll be watching the clock.
reaction.high: Rest matters — I appreciate that — but our supply window is closing too.

options.label: Descend carefully and deliberately, matching the team's pace.

reaction.text: That's the discipline that gets everyone home safe.
reaction.low: Acceptable. Discipline is what gets us home. Prove it.
reaction.high: That's the discipline that brings everyone home. Honestly — well led.

messages.text: Mission complete. Before we close — what mattered most to you this week?

reaction.text: That's the core of this whole mission. Well earned.
reaction.low: That's the core of it. Whether you actually led is a separate question.
reaction.high: That's the core of the mission — and honestly, you earned every word of it.

reaction.text: That's the lesson most people miss until it's too late. You got it early.
reaction.low: A fair lesson. Most learners say it — few actually mean it.
reaction.high: That's the lesson most people miss until it's too late. So glad you got it early.

options.label: Learning to make hard calls with incomplete information.

reaction.text: That's exactly what leadership at altitude demands.
reaction.low: Correct enough. Making them well is a different skill.
reaction.high: That's exactly what leadership at altitude demands. Well seen.

reflection.question: What will you carry forward from this expedition?

response.text: That's the foundation of everything we did. You saw it clearly.
response.low: A reasonable takeaway. It's the foundation, if you actually apply it.
response.high: That's the foundation of everything we did. Honestly — proud to have run logistics with you.

options.label: The discipline to know when to slow down.

response.text: That's the hardest lesson on this mountain. You learned it.
response.low: That's a hard lesson. Whether you truly learned it — the next mountain will show.
response.high: That's the hardest lesson on this mountain — and you learned it faster than most.

options.label: That leadership means trusting the right people at the right moment.

response.text: That's exactly the leader this mission needed. Well done.
response.low: That's a start. Whether you become that leader is up to you.
response.high: That's exactly the leader this mission needed. Honestly — I'd rope with you again.
